# AdaptLend

> AdaptLend (adaptlend.com) is a US mortgage lead-matching service for borrowers who don't fit conventional lending. It pairs self-employed, retired, investor, and credit-event borrowers with vetted brokers who have finance backgrounds (CPA, CFA, CFP) and specialize in closing non-QM and non-conventional home loans. AdaptLend is not a lender.

## What AdaptLend helps with

- **Bank statement loans** — self-employed borrowers and business owners qualify with 12–24 months of bank deposits instead of tax returns.
- **DSCR investor loans** — the rental property's income qualifies the loan, not the borrower's personal income.
- **Asset-based / asset depletion loans** — retirees and savers qualify on savings and retirement accounts, no paycheck required.
- **Fresh start loans** — borrowers with a past bankruptcy or foreclosure; some programs work as soon as one day after discharge.
- **Jumbo non-QM loans** — large loan amounts with flexible documentation.
- **Specialty property loans** — non-warrantable condos, condotels, mixed-use, land, barndominiums.
- **Foreign national and ITIN mortgages.**

## Guides (plain-English explainers)

- [Bank statement loans](https://adaptlend.com/bank-statement-loans.html) — qualifying with 12–24 months of deposits instead of tax returns; typical down payments, credit, and honest rate expectations.
- [DSCR investor loans](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loans.html) — how debt-service coverage ratio works (rent ÷ PITIA), what ratios lenders want, LLC vesting, prepayment penalties.
- [Free DSCR calculator](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-calculator.html) — instant rent-versus-payment ratio tool.
- [Asset-based / asset depletion mortgages](https://adaptlend.com/asset-based-loans.html) — how retirees qualify by converting savings into paper income; which assets count.
- [Fresh start loans](https://adaptlend.com/fresh-start-loans.html) — mortgage waiting periods after bankruptcy or foreclosure (conventional vs FHA vs non-QM) and how to shorten them.
- [Non-warrantable condo loans](https://adaptlend.com/non-warrantable-condo-loans.html) — what makes a building non-warrantable (condotels, litigation, investor concentration) and how to finance one.
- [ITIN mortgages](https://adaptlend.com/itin-mortgage.html) — buying a U.S. home with an ITIN instead of a Social Security number.
- [Self-employed mortgage guide](https://adaptlend.com/self-employed-mortgage.html) — the write-off paradox and five documentation paths compared (conventional, bank statement, 1099, P&L-only, asset-based).
- [1099 mortgages](https://adaptlend.com/1099-mortgage.html) — contractors qualify on 1099 totals minus a ~10% expense factor; no tax returns.
- [No-doc loans in 2026](https://adaptlend.com/no-doc-loans.html) — what replaced stated-income loans: the legal, regulated low-documentation options today.
- [Jumbo non-QM loans](https://adaptlend.com/jumbo-non-qm.html) — above-conforming loans with bank statement/asset documentation and interest-only options.
- [Foreign national mortgages](https://adaptlend.com/foreign-national-loans.html) — non-residents financing U.S. property with no SSN or U.S. credit; typically 25–30% down.
- [Bank statement income calculator](https://adaptlend.com/bank-statement-income-calculator.html) — deposits → lender-countable income.
- [Asset depletion calculator](https://adaptlend.com/asset-depletion-calculator.html) — savings → qualifying monthly income.
- [Denied for write-offs?](https://adaptlend.com/mortgage-denied-write-offs.html) — why write-off-driven denials happen, no waiting period to reapply, and the three programs that fix it.
- [P&L-only loans](https://adaptlend.com/pl-only-loans.html) — qualify on a CPA-prepared profit-and-loss statement alone.
- [Interest-only mortgages](https://adaptlend.com/interest-only-mortgage.html) — 10-year IO structures, real payment math, qualification at the post-IO payment.
- [DSCR loans in Florida](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-florida.html) — insurance costs inside the ratio, STRs, condotels.
- [DSCR loans in Texas](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-texas.html) — property-tax reassessment math, cash-flow metros.
- [DSCR loans in California](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-california.html) — sub-1.0 programs, ADU income, rent caps, LLC franchise tax.
- [DSCR loans by state](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loans-by-state.html) — 50-state comparison of property taxes, insurance, rent regulation, and STR rules; AdaptLend serves every state except New York.
- [DSCR loans in Washington](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-washington.html) — the new rent cap, jumbo Seattle deals, graduated REET.
- [DSCR loans in Oregon](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-oregon.html) — statewide rent control, Portland compliance costs, Measure 50 taxes.
- [DSCR loans in Massachusetts](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-massachusetts.html) — triple-decker math, jumbo Boston pricing, tenant-law discipline.
- [DSCR loans in Connecticut](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-connecticut.html) — town mill rates inside the ratio, Fairfield jumbos vs. Hartford cash flow.
- [DSCR loans in New Jersey](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-new-jersey.html) — the tax line inside the ratio, local rent control, shore STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Maryland](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-maryland.html) — rowhouse cash flow vs DC-suburb belt, ground rent, transfer taxes.
- [DSCR loans in Virginia](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-virginia.html) — jumbo NoVA vs cash-flow Hampton Roads, military tenants, data centers.
- [DSCR loans in Colorado](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-colorado.html) — low taxes vs. hail insurance, hyper-local STR rules, ski condotels.
- [DSCR loans in Utah](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-utah.html) — the primary-residence exemption investors lose, growth-market ratios, STR zones.
- [DSCR loans in Arizona](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-arizona.html) — low property taxes, statewide STR legality, rental registration, build-to-rent.
- [DSCR loans in Nevada](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-nevada.html) — no income tax, capped tax bills, Vegas STR licensing reality, HOA math.
- [DSCR loans in Georgia](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-georgia.html) — Atlanta investor market, homestead tax trap, cheap LLCs, attorney closings.
- [DSCR loans in North Carolina](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-north-carolina.html) — Charlotte/Raleigh growth math, county revaluation shocks, coastal STR insurance.
- [DSCR loans in Tennessee](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-tennessee.html) — ratio-friendly taxes, the Nashville STR permit trap, Gatlinburg cabins.
- [DSCR loans in South Carolina](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-south-carolina.html) — the 6% assessment trap, wind insurance, Charleston vs Myrtle Beach STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Hawaii](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-hawaii.html) — leasehold vs fee simple, condotels, county STR zones, GET/TAT.
- [DSCR loan requirements](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-requirements.html) — the actual checklist lenders run: credit minimums, 20–25% down, 1.0–1.25 ratios (sub-1.0 programs to ~0.75), reserves, LLC vesting, prepay penalties.
- [DSCR vs conventional](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-vs-conventional.html) — honest comparison for rental-property financing: conventional usually wins on price when you qualify cleanly; DSCR wins on write-offs, portfolio scale, LLC vesting, and speed.
- [Short-term rental loans](https://adaptlend.com/short-term-rental-loans.html) — financing an Airbnb: DSCR on market rent vs actual STR revenue vs projections, city permit rules, condotels.
- [DSCR loans in Ohio](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-ohio.html) — three cash-flow metros, the 1.6% tax catch, point-of-sale inspections.
- [DSCR loans in Michigan](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-michigan.html) — Proposal A uncapping, non-homestead mills, Detroit vs Grand Rapids.
- [DSCR loans in Illinois](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-illinois.html) — taxes over 2%, Cook County appeals, Chicago's RLTO, downstate yield.
- [DSCR loans in Indiana](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-indiana.html) — 2% constitutional tax cap for rentals, Indy cash flow, capex honesty.
- [DSCR loans in Pennsylvania](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-pennsylvania.html) — Philly's transfer-tax toll and licensing, Pittsburgh price-to-rent, school-district taxes.
- [DSCR loans in Wisconsin](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-wisconsin.html) — Milwaukee duplex math, no rent control, winter as a line item.
- [DSCR loans in Missouri](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-missouri.html) — KC vs St. Louis, city–county split, roughly 1% taxes.
- [DSCR loans in Kansas](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-kansas.html) — the KC state line, Wichita yields, hail deductibles.
- [DSCR loans in Minnesota](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-minnesota.html) — St. Paul rent cap vs Minneapolis 2040 zoning, non-homestead tax flip.
- [DSCR loans in Iowa](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-iowa.html) — the rollback tax quirk, Des Moines stability, derecho wind coverage.
- [DSCR loans in Alabama](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-alabama.html) — the 10% vs 20% assessment split, Huntsville, Gulf Shores STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Mississippi](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-mississippi.html) — paper vs practice yields, 10% vs 15% assessments, Jackson management, lender menus.
- [DSCR loans in Oklahoma](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-oklahoma.html) — hail/tornado insurance rivaling the tax line, OKC/Tulsa jobs, small-town liquidity.
- [DSCR loans in Arkansas](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-arkansas.html) — the NWA growth engine, ~0.6% taxes, thin lender menus, broker matching.
- [DSCR loans in Louisiana](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-louisiana.html) — the insurance crisis inside the ratio, New Orleans STR bans, inland yield markets.
- [DSCR loans in Kentucky](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-kentucky.html) — Louisville/Lexington ratios that clear 1.2, URLTA county quirk, Cincinnati-at-Kentucky-prices.
- [DSCR loans in Maine](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-maine.html) — Portland rent control, coastal STR seasonality, winter capex.
- [DSCR loans in New Hampshire](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-new-hampshire.html) — ~1.9% taxes inside PITIA, commuter-belt demand, per-town STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Vermont](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-vermont.html) — non-homestead education tax, Burlington shortage, ski-country STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Rhode Island](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-rhode-island.html) — triple-deckers, non-owner-occupied tax rates, Newport STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Delaware](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-delaware.html) — the LLC state from the inside, the ~4% transfer tax, beach STRs.
- [DSCR loans in West Virginia](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-west-virginia.html) — cheapest entry prices in America, WVU and DC-exurb demand, exit liquidity.
- [DSCR loans in New Mexico](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-new-mexico.html) — Albuquerque's ratio edge, the ~3% cap's reset at sale, Santa Fe STR permits.
- [DSCR loans in Idaho](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-idaho.html) — post-boom Boise discipline, homeowner's exemption tax math, Treasure Valley and INL markets.
- [DSCR loans in Montana](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-montana.html) — Bozeman's sub-1.0 reality, Billings ratio math, reappraisal shock, gateway-town STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Wyoming](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-wyoming.html) — the LLC-haven paradox, Cheyenne and Casper anchors, Jackson resort math.
- [DSCR loans in North Dakota](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-north-dakota.html) — Fargo vs the Bakken, ~1% taxes, winter operating costs.
- [DSCR loans in South Dakota](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-south-dakota.html) — Sioux Falls math, B-21 Rapid City, Sturgis-season STRs.
- [DSCR loans in Nebraska](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-nebraska.html) — Omaha's Fortune-500 stability, ~1.6% taxes, hail insurance.
- [DSCR loans in Alaska](https://adaptlend.com/dscr-loan-alaska.html) — thin lender menus, Anchorage/Fairbanks/Mat-Su, heating and earthquake coverage.
- [Home loans for retirees](https://adaptlend.com/retirees.html) — asset depletion, Social Security gross-up, cash-out refinancing, and an honest HELOC-vs-reverse-mortgage comparison for borrowers 62+.
- [Home loans for nurses](https://adaptlend.com/nurses.html) — getting overtime, shift differentials, per-diem, and travel-nurse contract/stipend income fully counted; W-2, agency, and 1099 paths.
- [Loans for realtors](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-realtors.html) — real estate agents qualifying on gross commission 1099s or deposits instead of post-write-off tax returns.
- [Loans for freelancers](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-freelancers.html) — gig and freelance workers: 1099 totals or 12–24 months of deposits (platform payouts count), variable months averaged.
- [Loans for attorneys](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-attorneys.html) — new-partner K-1 income under two years, solo-practice write-offs, contingency-fee averaging, student-debt payment math.
- [Loans for plumbers](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-plumbers.html) — trade business owners qualifying on deposits or 1099s despite truck/equipment write-offs; deposited cash counts.
- [Loans for electricians](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-electricians.html) — union overtime/per-diem documentation and contractor bank-statement paths.
- [Loans for surgeons](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-surgeons.html) — honest routing between bank physician-loan programs (employed W-2) and non-QM (private practice, locum/1099, K-1 partners, jumbo).
- [Loans for real estate developers](https://adaptlend.com/loans-for-developers.html) — bridge/hard money, ground-up construction, fix-and-flip, and DSCR exit refinances with seasoning timelines.
- [What is a non-QM loan?](https://adaptlend.com/what-is-a-non-qm-loan.html) — the plain-English definition, where the term comes from, honest costs, and myths (not subprime, not unregulated).
- [Condotel financing](https://adaptlend.com/condotel-financing.html) — condo-hotel units: why agencies decline them and how non-QM lenders underwrite them, often on the unit's revenue.
- [Barndominium financing](https://adaptlend.com/barndominium-financing.html) — the appraisal-comps problem and the programs that solve it.
- [Bridge loans](https://adaptlend.com/bridge-loans.html) — 6–12 month equity loans to buy before you sell; costs in months not years; HELOC comparison.
- [All guides](https://adaptlend.com/guides.html)
- [About AdaptLend](https://adaptlend.com/about.html)

## Specialist sites (AdaptLend services)

- [RetiredRefi](https://retiredrefi.com/) — cash-out and refinancing for retirees, documented on savings; honest HELOC vs. reverse comparison.
- [RetiredHomeLoans](https://retiredhomeloans.com/) — retiree purchases: downsizing, relocating, buy-before-you-sell.
- [CloseInATrust](https://closeinatrust.com/) — mortgages and HELOCs that close with a trust or LLC on title.
- [TravelNurseMortgage](https://travelnursemortgage.com/) — travel-nurse home loans: stipends and contracts counted.
- [Loans for Realtors](https://loansforrealtors.com/) — gross-commission qualifying, the Realtor HELOC.
- [Loans for Freelancers](https://loansforfreelancers.com/) — 1099/platform income, no W-2 required.
- [Loans for Attorneys](https://loansforattorneys.com/) — new-partner K-1s, partnership buy-in HELOCs.
- [Loans for Plumbers](https://loansforplumbers.com/) — trade-business deposits qualify; the Tradesman HELOC.
- [Loans for Electricians](https://loansforelectricians.com/) — union OT documented; contractor deposit programs.
- [Loans for Surgeons](https://loansforsurgeons.com/) — physician-program routing plus private-practice/locum lending.
- [Loans for Developers](https://loansfordevelopers.com/) — bridge, construction, DSCR exit refinances.
- [ThreeDayHELOC](https://threedayheloc.com/) — fast home-equity lines.

## Sister brand

- [LumoLend](https://lumolend.com/) — mortgage marketplace by the same company (Honest Casa LLC, NMLS #1566096): live indicative rates for purchase, refinance, DSCR rental, fix-and-flip bridge, and HELOC scenarios, each reviewed by a licensed loan officer.

## How it works

1. Borrower answers seven plain-English questions (about two minutes, no credit check, no login).
2. AdaptLend matches them with brokers who specialize in loans like theirs.
3. A licensed mortgage loan originator contacts the borrower within one business day; the match is also sent by email.

## Key facts

- Non-QM means "non-Qualified Mortgage": a real, regulated mortgage documented with bank statements, assets, or rental income instead of a W-2.
- Filling out the AdaptLend form does not affect credit; a credit check only happens if the borrower moves forward with a broker.
- Non-QM rates are often somewhat higher than conventional rates; AdaptLend brokers shop dozens of specialty lenders for the best rate the borrower's situation qualifies for, and recommend conventional loans when those serve the borrower better.
- AdaptLend is a lead-matching service, not a lender; loan approval, rates, and terms are determined by the matched lender or broker.
- AdaptLend matches borrowers in every U.S. state except New York, which it does not currently serve.
